welcome.title: Roszkowice Palace
welcome.text:
The palace in Roszkowice was built in the mid-19th century as the seat of the von Cramon-Taubadel family.
For decades it was the centre of estate life — a place of work, meetings and family events.

After the war the building served various utilitarian functions, gradually losing its representative character.
Today it is under renovation, aimed at preserving the monument and restoring its historical appearance.

We invite you to discover the history of the place, the people and the architecture that have shaped this palace for nearly 180 years.
